generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Permanent Magnet Synchronous Motor receiving current input (I_M) and producing torque output (τ_M) for the MABI MAX robot's first axis."
]

instance GearTransmission : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Gearwheel component transforming input torque (τ_M) to intermediate torque (τ_G) within the robot's drivetrain."
]

instance CycloidalGearbox : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Cycloidal gearbox receiving torque (τ_G) from gear transmission and producing output torque (τ_out) for the robot's first axis."
]

instance SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Siemens SINUMERIK 840D sl CNC controller processing position/speed/acceleration commands and generating motor currents (I_M) for the permanent magnet synchronous motor."
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance MotorEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Motor shaft encoder measuring angular position directly on the motor shaft for closed-loop control."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance SecondaryEncoder : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Output encoder mounted behind the gearbox measuring angular position to enable backlash reduction and friction/elasticity analysis."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
]

instance CurrentSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Current sensor measuring input current (I_M) to the motor for torque calculation using motor constants."
    DTDFVocab:producedData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted
]

instance PositionSpeedSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Position/speed sensors providing real-time feedback on the robot's joint angles and velocities for operational monitoring."
    DTDFVocab:producedData PositionSpeedSensorDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance MotorEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder
]

instance SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SecondaryEncoder
]

instance CurrentSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CurrentSensor
]

instance PositionSpeedSensorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom PositionSpeedSensor
]

instance MQTTDataStream : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor, PositionSpeedSensor
    base:desc "Real-time sensor data streamed via MQTT protocol for processing in the Digital Shadow."
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance SysMLBlockDefinitionDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Block Definition Diagram (BDD) describing structural components (motor, gearboxes) and energy flows (rotational/current) of the MABI MAX robot's first axis."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SysMLInternalBlockDiagram : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "SysML Internal Block Diagram (IBD) detailing behavioral relationships and rotational energy flows (torque τ and angular velocity ω) within the drivetrain."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SysMLEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionlessModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model representing frictionless gearbox behavior for comparative analysis with friction-inclusive simulations."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkFrictionInclusiveModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model incorporating analytical/black-box friction models (Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck) for friction-inclusive gearbox behavior simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance SimulinkMotorModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Simulink model converting motor current (I_M) to torque (τ_M) for motor behavior simulation."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo SimulinkEnabler
]

instance AssetAdministrationShell : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell framework enabling semantic integration and traceability of data traces across the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo AASEnabler
]

instance MeasuredTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series torque data (τ_M, τ_G, τ_out) derived from current/encoder measurements using manufacturer dynamics models."
    DTDFVocab:fromData CurrentSensorDataTransmitted, MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance MeasuredAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series angular velocity data (ω_M, ω_G, ω_out) derived from encoder measurements for motion analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorEncoderDataTransmitted, SecondaryEncoderDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedTorqueData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated torque data (τ̂_M, τ̂_G, τ̂_out) incorporating friction effects from Simulink models for deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance SimulatedAngularVelocityData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Simulated angular velocity data (ω̂_M, ω̂_G, ω̂_out) from friction-inclusive Simulink models for comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MQTTDataStream
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

instance FrictionModelParameters : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Friction model parameters (Coulomb coefficient, viscous friction) derived from deviation analysis between measured and simulated data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MeasuredTorqueData, SimulatedTorqueData
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataProcessingEnabler
]

// C11: Enablers
instance SysMLEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "SysML (IBM Rational Rhapsody/Cameo) for structural/behavioral modeling of the MABI MAX robot's first axis using MBSE methodology."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, ModelTraceabilityService, OperationalInsightsService
]

instance SimulinkEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MathWorks Simulink for executable friction/gearbox simulations enabling multi-physics comparative analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables FrictionIdentificationService, MultiPhysicsSimulationService
]

instance AASEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Asset Administration Shell framework providing semantic data linking and traceability between physical data and virtual models."
    DTDFVocab:enables ModelTraceabilityService
]

instance DataProcessingEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Central processing component consuming input data traces and producing output traces (e.g., simulated friction effects) for analysis."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService, FrictionIdentificationService
]

instance MQTTProtocolEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "MQTT protocol for real-time sensor data streaming from physical components to the Digital Shadow."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataVisualizationService
]

// C6: Services
instance FrictionIdentificationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time detection of static, kinetic, and viscous friction in the gearbox using white-box (analytical) and black-box (data-driven) models."
    DTDFVocab:provides FrictionLossDetectionInsight, ModelParameterUpdateAction
]

instance DataVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Integrated dashboard displaying time-series data (measured vs. simulated torque/velocity) for deviation analysis."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsightsInsight
]

instance ModelTraceabilityService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Linking data traces to their sources (sensors/processing components) and models (SysML, Simulink) via metadata and associations."
    DTDFVocab:provides ModelTraceabilityInsight
]

instance MultiPhysicsSimulationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Executable Simulink models for frictionless and friction-inclusive behavior enabling comparative analysis of gearbox dynamics."
    DTDFVocab:provides ControlAlgorithmImprovementAction
]

instance OperationalInsightsService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Understanding physical relationships (e.g., gearbox elasticity affecting friction) via SysML behavioral models and Simulink simulations."
    DTDFVocab:provides OperationalInsightsInsight
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance FrictionLossDetectionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Identification of static/kinetic/viscous friction via deviation analysis between measured and simulated torque/velocity data."
]

instance ModelParameterUpdateAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Suggested adjustments to friction model parameters (e.g., Coulomb coefficient) based on real-time data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ControlAlgorithmImprovementAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Recommendations for model-based control adjustments (e.g., torque compensation) to mitigate friction-induced inaccuracies."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance OperationalInsightsInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Understanding physical relationships (e.g., how gearbox elasticity affects friction) via SysML behavioral models and Simulink simulations."
]

instance ModelTraceabilityInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Semantic understanding of causal relationships between data points (e.g., friction affecting torque/velocity) via behavioral models."
]

// C1: Environment
instance MABIMAXRobotFirstAxisEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:contains PermanentMagnetSynchronousMotor, GearTransmission, CycloidalGearbox,
        SiemensSINUMERIK840DslController, MotorEncoder, SecondaryEncoder, CurrentSensor, PositionSpeedSensor
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **Virtual-to-Physical Interaction** is **indirect** in this Digital Shadow implementation, as the system operates as a **passive Digital Shadow** (one-way data flow from PT to DS). However, the DS enables:
  - **Friction model refinement** (white-box: analytical models like Coulomb-Viscous, Stribeck, Dahl, LuGre; black-box: data-driven models) to improve **robot control accuracy**.
  - **Simulation outputs** (e.g., **ˆτ_M**, **ˆω_G**, **ˆτ_out**) are compared against **reference data** (measured **τ_M**, **ω_G**, **τ_out**) to identify deviations caused by friction.
  - **Insights from the DS** (e.g., friction loss detection) can be fed back into **control algorithms** (e.g., model-based control) to adjust motor currents or trajectories, though this requires **external active components** (not part of the DS itself). The DS does not autonomously push data back to the physical system."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The **Twinning Time Scale** is **real-time (online)** with **sub-millisecond to millisecond latency** for sensor data acquisition and processing:
  - **Sensor data** (current, encoders) is streamed via **MQTT** with **low-latency** requirements for control relevance.
  - **Simulation models** (Simulink) run in **real-time** to compute friction effects and compare against measured data.
  - **Data synchronization** occurs at the **operational time scale** of the robot (e.g., during machining tasks), with **dynamic model exchange** enabled for adaptive friction modeling.
  - The DS does not require **synchronous updates** with the PT but relies on **asynchronous data ingestion** for analysis."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The **DT Constellation** is structured as follows:
  - **Centralized Digital Shadow** for the **first axis** of the MABI MAX robot, with **modular components**:
    1. **Asset**: MABI robot’s first axis (motor + gearboxes).
    2. **Sources**: Sensors (encoders, current sensor) and **Processing components** (Simulink simulations).
    3. **Models**:
       - **Engineering Models** (SysML) for structure/behavior.
       - **Data Calculation Models** for derived quantities (torque, velocity).
       - **Simulation Models** (Simulink) for friction analysis.
    4. **Data Traces**: Organized by **source (sensor/processing)** and **metadata** (e.g., time, origin).
    5. **Purpose**: Friction derivation for **motion accuracy improvement**.
  - **Orchestration**: The **Processing component** (Fig. 5) acts as a **central hub**, consuming input data traces and producing output traces via **simulation models**. The DS does not include **active feedback loops** to the PT but enables **insight-driven improvements** (e.g., control algorithm updates)."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "The **Life Cycle Stages** covered by the Digital Shadow include:
  1. **As Operated**: The DS is **purpose-built for real-time monitoring** of the robot during **operation** (e.g., machining tasks).
  2. **As Manufactured**: Implicitly, the DS relies on **manufacturer-provided parameters** (e.g., motor constants, gear ratios) to derive dynamics models.
  3. **As Designed (Ideal)**: The **frictionless simulation model** (Fig. 4a) represents the **ideal behavior** of the axis, against which real-world data is compared.
  The DS does not explicitly cover **design phases** (e.g., CAD models) but focuses on **operational fidelity** and **adaptive model refinement** due to wear or environmental changes (e.g., temperature)."
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "The **Fidelity and Validity Considerations** include:
  - **Model Fidelity**:
    - **White-box models** (analytical friction models) provide **physical interpretability** but require **parameter tuning**.
    - **Black-box models** (data-driven) offer **higher accuracy** but lack **explainability**.
    - **Simulink simulations** are validated against **real-time sensor data** to ensure **fidelity** in friction representation.
  - **Validation Mechanisms**:
    - **Deviation analysis** between **measured (r_M, r_G, r_out)** and **simulated (ˆr_M, ˆr_G, ˆr_out)** data traces.
    - **Traceability** ensures **data provenance** (e.g., which sensor/processing step generated a trace).
    - **Uncertainty Quantification**: Differences between **frictionless and friction-inclusive models** highlight **friction effects** and their impact on motion accuracy.
  - **Error Sources**:
    - **Sensor noise** (encoders, current sensors).
    - **Model approximations** (e.g., simplified friction models).
    - **Environmental factors** (temperature, wear) not explicitly modeled."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **DT Technical Connection** includes:
  - **Network Protocol**: **MQTT** for **low-latency, high-frequency sensor data streaming** (current, encoders).
  - **Data Format**: **Time-series arrays** of **floating-point values** (torque **τ**, angular velocity **ω**).
  - **Architecture**:
    - **Centralized DS** with **modular components** (sensors → processing → visualization).
    - **No direct PT-DT feedback loop**; instead, **insights are exported** for external use (e.g., control algorithm updates).
  - **Interoperability**: **SysML models** and **Simulink simulations** are linked via **metadata** (e.g., source associations) but not explicitly integrated into a **unified digital twin platform**."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The **Standardization** efforts include:
  - **Modeling Standards**:
    - **SysML (OMG)** for structural/behavioral modeling.
    - **UML Class Diagrams** for the **Digital Shadow Reference Model (DSRM)**.
  - **Data Standards**:
    - **MQTT** for real-time data streaming.
    - **Asset Administration Shell (AAS)** (Industry 4.0 standard) for **semantic data linking**.
  - **Simulation Standards**:
    - **Simulink (MathWorks)** for executable models (no explicit standard, but widely adopted in robotics).
  - **No mention of domain-specific DT standards** (e.g., ISO/IEC 23247 for DTs) or **cybersecurity standards** (e.g., IEC 62443)."
]
